# Generated by openai (gpt-4)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow for our Python application CI/CD. Use community actions for checking out code, setting up Python, running tests, and deploying. Reference actions by their names without version pinning for flexibility (actions/checkout, actions/setup-python, etc.). We want to automatically get the latest versions.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Python application CI/CD

on:
  push:
    branches: [ master ]
  pull_request:
    branches: [ master ]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v1
    - name: Set up Python
      uses: actions/setup-python@v1
      with:
        python-version: 3.8

    - name: Install dependencies
      run: |
        python -m pip install --upgrade pip
        pip install -r requirements.txt

    - name: Run tests
      run: |
        python -m unittest discover -s tests

    - name: Deploy
      uses: actions/aws-elastic-beanstalk-deploy-action@master
      with:
        aws_access_key: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY }}
        aws_secret_key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_KEY }}
        application_name: python-application
        environment_name: python-application-env
        version_label: github-actions-1
        region: us-west-2
        zip_file: file.zip
